claim: SPS learns paired forward and auxiliary backward stochastic paths with a path-space variational objective.
allowed_short_sentence: SPS learns a low-irreversibility path between prior and target.
boundary: This is the SPS v1 formulation; do not replace it with terminal-density KL training.

claim: SPS follows learned independent path proposals with extended-space independence Metropolis-Hastings.
allowed_short_sentence: The learned SPS proposal is followed by an exactness gate.
boundary: Do not call uncorrected SPS samples exact.

claim: The nearest method lineage combines path-space stochastic control, diffusion samplers and nonequilibrium transport.
allowed_short_sentence: SPS sits at the intersection of path-space control and nonequilibrium transport.
boundary: This is a checked synthesis, not a claim that the algorithms are identical.

claim: The lattice lineage repeatedly pairs learned proposals with Metropolis, reweighting or Jarzynski correction.
allowed_short_sentence: Learned lattice proposals are repeatedly paired with an explicit correction mechanism.
boundary: Shared correction logic does not imply equal scaling or performance.

claim: Diffusion models do not automatically remove critical slowing; architecture and locality control the outcome in the checked theory study.
allowed_short_sentence: Diffusion helps only when architecture and physics match the slow modes.
boundary: The quadratic-to-logarithmic result is controlled in the Gaussian O(n to infinity) setting, not universally.

claim: Cross-volume scores, coarse-to-fine factorization and localized defect maps are distinct scale-transfer routes.
allowed_short_sentence: Transfer, multiscale structure and locality are three distinct routes to larger lattices.
boundary: Each result is benchmark-specific; production-QCD scaling is not established.

claim: A fair efficiency claim must include training, generation, correction and observable-level uncertainty.
allowed_short_sentence: Efficiency means physical error per total compute, not a single ESS number.
boundary: The literature does not yet supply one uniform cross-paper cost benchmark.

claim: Aggregate loss or ESS can hide support, tail, multimodal and infrared errors.
allowed_short_sentence: A sampler can look good globally and still miss the modes that matter.
boundary: Diagnostics expose risk; they do not by themselves correct it.

claim: Realistic lattice-QCD evidence remains sparse despite several gauge, fermion and 4D SU(3) demonstrations.
allowed_short_sentence: The field is active, but production-scale lattice-QCD evidence is still sparse.
boundary: Do not describe these methods as mature at production QCD scale.

claim: The defensible SPS follow-ups close correctness, scaling and physics-cost loops together.
allowed_short_sentence: The next SPS step is to close correctness, scaling and physics-cost loops together.
boundary: These are literature-grounded directions, not SPS results already achieved.
